title: Learning a New Language
summary: A narrator shares his experience of learning Spanish, describing his methods, challenges, and the progress he has made over several months.
body: About six months ago, I decided to start learning Spanish. I had always wanted to speak another language, and Spanish seemed like a great choice because so many people speak it around the world. At first, it was quite difficult. The grammar was confusing, and I made many mistakes when I tried to speak. However, I did not give up. I started using a language learning app on my phone every morning before work. The app gave me short lessons with listening exercises, vocabulary games, and simple conversations. After a few weeks, I began to recognise words I had heard before, and that felt really good. I also joined an online group where people practise Spanish together. We meet twice a week on video call and chat about different topics. Sometimes I still make mistakes, but everyone in the group is very supportive and patient. My biggest challenge was learning new vocabulary because there are so many words to remember. So I started writing new words in a small notebook and reviewing them on the bus or during lunch. Now, after six months, I can have basic conversations and understand Spanish TV shows when the subtitles are on. Learning a language takes time and patience, but every small step forward feels like a real achievement.

annotations.meaningVi: có vẻ là một lựa chọn tuyệt vời
annotations.exampleEn: Studying engineering seemed like a great choice for him.
Don't give up — you're almost there!
She uses a language learning app to practise French every day.
The textbook includes listening exercises with audio recordings.
I can recognise his voice even on the phone.
Good teachers are supportive and patient with their students.
The biggest challenge was managing my time at university.
She spent the evening reviewing her notes before the exam.

annotations.meaningVi: các cuộc trò chuyện cơ bản
annotations.exampleEn: After two months, he could hold basic conversations in Italian.
I watch foreign films with subtitles to help me understand.
Finishing the marathon was a real achievement for her.

grammarNotes.title: However — connecting contrasting ideas
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'However' được dùng để kết nối hai ý trái ngược nhau, thường đứng đầu câu tiếp theo sau dấu chấm hoặc sau dấu phẩy. Ví dụ: 'The grammar was confusing. However, I did not give up.' Nó tương tự như 'but' nhưng trang trọng hơn một chút.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'However' connects two contrasting ideas. It usually starts a new sentence or follows a semicolon. Example: 'The grammar was confusing. However, I did not give up.' It is similar to 'but' but slightly more formal and emphasises the contrast more strongly.

grammarNotes.title: After + -ing clause for sequential actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'After + V-ing' diễn tả một hành động xảy ra trước một hành động khác. Ví dụ: 'After a few weeks, I began to recognise words' — hành động nhận ra từ xảy ra sau khoảng thời gian học. Cấu trúc này tránh lặp lại chủ ngữ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'after + -ing' to show that one action happens before another. Example: 'After a few weeks, I began to recognise words.' The -ing form (a gerund phrase) refers to the same subject as the main clause, making the sentence concise.

translationVi: Khoảng sáu tháng trước, tôi quyết định bắt đầu học tiếng Tây Ban Nha. Tôi luôn muốn nói được một ngôn ngữ khác, và tiếng Tây Ban Nha có vẻ là một lựa chọn tuyệt vời vì có rất nhiều người nói ngôn ngữ này trên thế giới. Lúc đầu, khá khó khăn. Ngữ pháp rất khó hiểu và tôi mắc nhiều lỗi khi cố nói. Tuy nhiên, tôi không bỏ cuộc. Tôi bắt đầu dùng ứng dụng học ngôn ngữ trên điện thoại mỗi sáng trước khi đi làm. Ứng dụng cung cấp các bài học ngắn với bài tập nghe, trò chơi từ vựng và các cuộc trò chuyện đơn giản. Sau vài tuần, tôi bắt đầu nhận ra những từ đã nghe trước đây, và điều đó cảm thấy rất tốt. Tôi cũng tham gia một nhóm trực tuyến nơi mọi người cùng luyện tiếng Tây Ban Nha. Chúng tôi gặp nhau hai lần một tuần qua video call và nói chuyện về các chủ đề khác nhau. Đôi khi tôi vẫn mắc lỗi, nhưng mọi người trong nhóm rất ủng hộ và kiên nhẫn. Thử thách lớn nhất của tôi là học từ vựng mới vì có quá nhiều từ cần nhớ. Vì vậy, tôi bắt đầu ghi từ mới vào một cuốn sổ nhỏ và ôn lại trên xe buýt hoặc trong giờ nghỉ trưa. Bây giờ, sau sáu tháng, tôi có thể nói các cuộc trò chuyện cơ bản và hiểu các chương trình truyền hình tiếng Tây Ban Nha khi bật phụ đề. Học ngôn ngữ cần thời gian và sự kiên nhẫn, nhưng mỗi bước tiến nhỏ đều cảm thấy như một thành tích thực sự.

questions.prompt: How long ago did the narrator start learning Spanish?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'About six months ago, I decided to start learning Spanish.'
questions.hint: Listen for a specific time period.

questions.prompt: Why did the narrator choose Spanish?
options: Because many people speak it around the world
Because it was required for work
questions.explanation: He says Spanish 'seemed like a great choice because so many people speak it around the world.'
questions.hint: He mentions the global popularity of the language.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator find difficult at first?
questions.explanation: He says 'The grammar was confusing, and I made many mistakes.'
questions.hint: He mentions a specific aspect of the language that confused him.

questions.prompt: When does the narrator use his language learning app?
questions.explanation: He says 'I started using a language learning app on my phone every morning before work.'
questions.hint: The time is before something else happens.

questions.prompt: What does the app include? (Choose the option that lists three things from the text.)
options: Grammar lessons, reading tests, and writing exercises
Listening exercises, vocabulary games, and simple conversations
Speaking tests, grammar notes, and translation tasks
Video lessons, quizzes, and grammar charts
questions.explanation: The app gave him 'listening exercises, vocabulary games, and simple conversations.'

questions.prompt: After a few weeks, what could the narrator do?
options: Recognise words he had heard before
questions.explanation: He says 'I began to recognise words I had heard before, and that felt really good.'

questions.prompt: What is the online group the narrator joined for?
questions.explanation: He joined 'an online group where people practise Spanish together.'
questions.hint: It is a group practice activity.

questions.prompt: How often does the group meet?
questions.explanation: He says 'We meet twice a week on video call.'
questions.hint: They meet more than once but less than three times a week.

questions.prompt: How do the people in the group treat the narrator when he makes mistakes?
questions.explanation: He says 'everyone in the group is very supportive and patient.'
questions.hint: Two positive adjectives describe their attitude.

questions.prompt: What was the narrator's biggest challenge?
questions.explanation: He says 'My biggest challenge was learning new vocabulary because there are so many words to remember.'
questions.hint: He struggles with the number of words.

questions.prompt: How does the narrator record new vocabulary?
options: He types it into his phone
He writes it in a small notebook
He records it as audio notes
questions.explanation: He 'started writing new words in a small notebook.'
questions.hint: He uses a physical writing tool.

questions.prompt: After six months, what can the narrator do?
options: Have basic conversations and understand Spanish TV with subtitles
questions.explanation: He can 'have basic conversations and understand Spanish TV shows when the subtitles are on.'
questions.hint: He can do two things, but still needs some help with TV.

blanks.explanation: 'Spanish' — the narrator specifically chose Spanish as the language to learn.
'choice' — Spanish seemed like a great choice because so many people speak it.
'difficult' — at first it was quite difficult.
'mistakes' — he made many mistakes when he tried to speak.
'app' — he used a language learning app on his phone.
'recognise' — he began to recognise words he had heard before.
'group' — he joined an online group where people practise Spanish together.
'twice' — they meet twice a week on video call.
'vocabulary' — his biggest challenge was learning new vocabulary.
'notebook' — he writes new words in a small notebook.
'subtitles' — he can understand Spanish TV shows when the subtitles are on.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says that every small step forward feels like a real achievement when learning a language. What strategies do you think are most effective for learning a new language, and how do you stay motivated when progress feels slow?
